title: Share of population with cancer
subtitle: The estimated share of population with any form of [cancer](#dod:cancer).
note: [Non-melanoma skin cancers](#dod:non-melanoma-skin-cancer) are excluded due to potentially incomplete records and inconsistent registry practices.
caption:
Share of population with cancer

The estimated share of population with any form of cancer.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated share of the population with any form of cancer in 2023, excluding non-melanoma skin cancers due to potentially incomplete records and inconsistent registry practices. The legend ranges from 0% to 4%, with color bands marking intervals at 0.5%, 1%, 1.5%, 2%, 2.5%, 3%, and 3.5%. The darkest red shades, indicating shares above 3.5% and approaching 4%, are visible in the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and several countries in Western and Northern Europe including the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and the Nordic nations. A broad band of mid-range orange shades, falling roughly between 1.5% and 3%, covers much of Eastern Europe, Russia, and parts of South America such as Argentina and Uruguay. Lighter beige and pale orange tones, representing shares below 1.5%, dominate the African continent, the Middle East, and most of South and Southeast Asia. Many countries in Central Africa and parts of Asia appear in the lightest shade, near the 0% to 0.5% range. Greenland and a few small island territories show hatching or very pale coloring, suggesting no data or minimal recorded prevalence. The visual pattern shows the highest estimated shares concentrated in high-income regions of North America, Europe, and Oceania, while lower shares are distributed across much of Africa, Asia, and Latin America.
